laws.title: Public Law 9-62 | Repealing the District of Columbia Home Rule Act
laws.status_basis: Operative local/District repeal act; it repeals the D.C. Home Rule Act and does not create a new positive-law section or self-repeal.

provisions.exact_change: Keep only a concise short-title note.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the "Repealing the District of Columbia Home Rule Act."

provisions.text_summary: Repeals the District of Columbia Home Rule Act of 2010 and restores or revives law amended or repealed by it.
provisions.exact_change: Local/District repeal and revival clause; no U.S. Code section is directly amended.
provisions.evidence: The District of Columbia Home Rule Act of 2010 (Pub.L. 93-198) is repealed, and the provisions of law amended or repealed by that Act are restored or revived as if the Act had not been enacted.
provisions.notes: Keep, at most, a brief historical note.

current_implementation.detail: The current note sits in the D.C. charter subject area, but it still carries a source dump and should be reduced to a concise repeal-history entry.

recommended_actions: Trim the note to a concise repeal-history statement.
Remove the quoted source dump and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 9-64 | To Bar T800Ninja123 From Public Office
laws.status_basis: Operative individual barment resolution; it takes effect by its terms and does not self-repeal or expire.

provisions.exact_change: Keep only a concise short-title note.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the "HJR 1 - To Bar T800Ninja123FromPublic Office".

provisions.exact_change: Non-code clause; do not convert to U.S. Code text.
provisions.evidence: This Act shall be severable ... This Act shall not be superseded by any newer law unless that law specifically declares its intent to supersede this Act's provisions.

provisions.text_summary: Immediate effective date after both chambers approve it.
provisions.exact_change: Non-code effective-date clause.
provisions.evidence: This Act shall become effective following a majority approval by both the House of Representatives and Senate.

provisions.exact_change: Pure documentation clause; no U.S. Code treatment.
provisions.evidence: T800Ninja123 shall be known as here

provisions.text_summary: Bars T800Ninja123 from public office and imposes related employment restrictions.
provisions.exact_change: Individual/private barment measure; keep out of the U.S. Code.
provisions.evidence: T800Ninja123, shall be hereby barred from holding any public office under the seal of the United States.
provisions.notes: This is a named-person measure and should remain historical only.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note is anchored in Title 46 and carries a full-text dump plus Trello link. This is a private/individual barment measure, so it does not belong in permanent U.S. Code text.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link.
Exclude this private/individual measure from permanent U.S. Code text; keep only a short historical note if needed.

laws.title: Public Law 10-67 | Ambassadorial Readjustment Act
laws.status_basis: General foreign-affairs organizational legislation; the source creates permanent ambassadorial rules and does not self-repeal or expire.

provisions.exact_change: Keep only a concise short-title note.
provisions.evidence: This act shall be referred to as the "Ambassadorial Readjustment Act".

provisions.text_summary: Creates ambassador nomination and acting rules, allows multi-portfolio assignments, sets portfolio geography, establishes five ambassadorial portfolios, gives the UN ambassador Under Secretary seniority, and requires notice to the Secretary of State.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new Title 22 provision covering ambassador nomination, interim service, multiple portfolios, portfolio assignment, and the five named portfolios.
provisions.evidence: The President shall nominate Ambassadors to be commissioned by and with the consent of the Senate. ... The following portfolios shall be established by law:
provisions.notes: This is substantive permanent foreign-affairs law.

provisions.text_summary: Blanket repeal of conflicting laws and Code provisions; immediate effective date.
provisions.exact_change: Treat the conflict clause as a source-defect, nondeterminable repeal note; do not invent specific strike text.
provisions.evidence: Any act, part of the United States Code, or law that may come into conflict with clauses mentioned under Section 2 are to be rescinded and repealed immediately.

current_implementation.detail: The current treatment is only a boilerplate note with a dump and Trello link; the substantive foreign-affairs rules still need real codification.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link.
Codify the substantive Section 2 rules as a new Title 22 provision near 22 U.S.C. 2651a.
Treat the Section 3 conflict clause as a nondeterminable statutory note only.

laws.title: Public Law 11-70 | Supreme Court Please Act
laws.status_basis: Operative transfer statute; it transfers Supreme Court Police duties to the U.S. Marshals Service and does not self-repeal or expire.

provisions.exact_change: Keep only a concise short-title note.
provisions.evidence: This act shall be referred to as the "Supreme Court Please Act"

provisions.exact_change: Transfer the duties tied to 40 U.S.C. 6121 to the U.S. Marshals Service and terminate the Supreme Court Police workforce.
provisions.evidence: All duties of the Marshal of the United States Supreme Court and the Supreme Court Police (40U.S.Code 6121) shall be transferred to the United States Marshals Service. ... All employees of the Supreme Court Police shall be terminated.

provisions.exact_change: Keep as a short effective-date note.

laws.title: Public Law 11-71 | Supreme Court Needs Act
laws.status_basis: Section 2(a) repeals 28 U.S.C. section 1 and section 2(b) immediately replaces it with new text setting the Court at a Chief Justice plus four associate justices. The source contains no self-repeal or sunset.

provisions.evidence: This act shall be referred to as the Supreme Court Needs Act.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals 28 U.S.C. section 1 and replaces it with new Supreme Court membership and quorum text.
provisions.exact_change: Replace 28 U.S.C. section 1 with text stating that the Supreme Court consists of a Chief Justice and four associate justices and may determine quorum.
provisions.evidence: 28 U.S. Code section 1 is repealed. 28 U.S. Code section 1 shall read as: The Supreme Court of the United States shall consist of a Chief Justice of the United States and four associate justices.
provisions.notes: This is a determinable strike-and-replace amendment, not a note.

provisions.evidence: This act shall immediately take effect upon passage.

current_implementation.detail: The note is parked at the right anchor but remains boilerplate with a dump and Trello link; the actual section text still needs replacement.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link.
Actually amend 28 U.S.C. 1 to the new Chief Justice plus four associate justices text.
